title: Dracocephalum fragrans
intro: Dracocephalum fragrans, also known as Dragon's Breath or Fragrant Dragon Head, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Dracocephalum fragrans include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Dracocephalum fragrans is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is a member of the genus Dracocephalum, which is a group of plants native to Asia and Europe. The genus Dracocephalum is further classified under the family Lamiaceae, which consists of over 7,000 species of flowering plants. The Lamiaceae family is a part of the order Lamiales, which includes a diverse range of plant species. The order Lamiales is a subgroup of the larger class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledons.
